pages.raw_text:
Yesterday was the 18th anniver-
sary of the big fire that wiped out
the business district of Spokane.
The date occasioned great deal Of
Renaissance among citizens who
were here at the time.
c. F. Waldron, of Occur d'Alene,
found his little son and daughter
here yesterday in care of Attorney
Fred Miller. He had expected
them on another train from Boise
and failed to meet them. Miller was
coming home and assumed charge
Of the youngsters. Their mother is
dead.


Teachers examinations for state
and county certificates begin today
in the county superintendent Office
at the courthouse. Tie first days
will be devoted to state examina-
tions and the next 3 to county
grades.


DR.. w. H. Appleton and Miss
! Adelaide Laird, of the Shirley Co.,
were married yesterday at Occur
d'Alene, Idaho. Miss Laird played
her part as leading lady with the
Shirleys Saturday night.
Miss Adeline Smith, who will
leave in September for missionary
work In China, addressed the Wom-
